message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>As the COVID-19 pandemic wanes, states may reintroduce Medicaid work requirements to reduce enrollment. Using the Health and Retirement Study, we evaluated chronic disease burden among beneficiaries aged >50 (n=1460) who might be impacted by work requirements (i.e. working <20 hours per week). Seven of eight chronic conditions evaluated were associated with reduced workforce participation, including history of stroke (OR: 7.35; 95% CI: 2.98-18.14) and lung disease (OR: 4.39; 95% CI: 2.97-7.47). Those with more severe disease were also more likely to work fewer hours. Medicaid work requirements would likely have great impact on older beneficiaries with significant disease burden.</jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Key Points</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Chronic disease linked to reduced work among older Medicaid beneficiaries.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Work requirements would greatly impact those aged >50 with chronic conditions.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Coverage loss would have negative implications for long-term disease management.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Chronic Disease and Workforce Participation Among Medicaid Enrollees Over 50: The Potential Impact of Medicaid Work Requirements Post-COVID-19
